name: Gasconade River
description: The longest river entirely within Missouri (~280 mi) and 'one of the world's crookedest', a winding low-gradient Ozark float stream that rises in the southeastern Webster/Wright County Ozarks and flows generally north-northeast through the Salem Plateau to the Missouri River at Gasconade. Incised meanders and scenic dolostone/sandstone bluffs (the plateau stands ~300 ft above the river near Waynesville); numerous springs and caves. Difficulty Class I–II (II seldom); a good, uncrowded float stream — common to go miles without seeing another boat. Largemouth and smallmouth bass. Major tributaries: Osage Fork, Roubidoux Creek, and Big Piney River. Spring-influenced but runoff-responsive; long floatable season; many low-water access bridges. Long enough to warrant multiple calibration reaches (upper near Hazelgreen, middle at Jerome, lower near Rich Fountain).
managingAuthority: Missouri Department of Conservation (state-managed accesses); streambed navigability per Missouri law

accessLaw.value: Missouri follows the "navigable in fact" doctrine: the public may float and wade streams navigable in fact, but streambeds/banks are frequently privately owned, so access is legal from public road crossings, public land, and established accesses, while portaging or camping on private gravel bars can be trespass. Navigability is decided stream by stream; there is no statewide list. (Shared Missouri paragraph — confirm the AGO/MDC citation once for the state.)
accessLaw.source: https://mdc.mo.gov/ (MDC stream access) — confirm AGO citation once for MO

sections.name: Upper Gasconade (Hazelgreen area)
sections.description: Upper reach represented by the Hazelgreen gauge. Winding, low-gradient, spring-influenced Ozark float with smallmouth fishing.

representativeGauge.rationale: Hazelgreen (06928000) is the upper-river reference gauge with an NWS forecast point (HZLM7)

thresholds.source: rivers.moherp.org Missouri paddler gauge ratings, Hazelgreen 06928000 page (owner-provided capture 2026-07-06): OBSERVED (community trip-calibrated) thresholds — Low 100 cfs.
rivers.moherp.org Hazelgreen OBSERVED rating: Good begins at 200 cfs ('ideal for a canoe or kayak; boats drag on fewer than one-quarter of riffles').
corroboratingSources: moherp trip reports rated Good at 443 cfs/1.88 ft (2018-09-15 Cave Lodge→Ruby's Landing), 616 cfs/2.48 ft (2010-06-12 Osage Fork→Hazelgreen), 1230 cfs/3.10 ft (2026-05-04 Blacks Ford→Gasconade Hills Resort)

thresholds.source: rivers.moherp.org Hazelgreen OBSERVED rating: High begins at 1000 cfs (top of the Good band).
rivers.moherp.org Hazelgreen OBSERVED rating: High 1000 cfs (site taxonomy: 'high and potentially dangerous… canoe travel may not be advisable').
corroboratingSources: moherp trips rated High at 1090 cfs/3.16 ft (2016-10-23) and 3350 cfs/5.78 ft (2022-04-23). CONTRARY datum: one 2019-05-27 trip at 2840 cfs was logged Good — the 1000-cfs boundary is soft; owner review at signoff.

publishedFloatTimes.source: rivers.moherp.org trip reports (2019-05-27, 2022-04-23), Hazelgreen gauge page

publishedFloatTimes.reachLabel: Blacks Ford to Gasconade Hills Resort
publishedFloatTimes.source: rivers.moherp.org trip report (2026-05-04), Hazelgreen gauge page
rivers.moherp.org trip reports (2010-06-12, 2016-10-23), Hazelgreen gauge page
rivers.moherp.org trip report (2018-09-15), Hazelgreen gauge page

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN (numeric, gauge-keyed) — no cfs navigability table captured yet (the Agnew narrative provided character but not the table). CRITICAL CAVEAT: the upper Gasconade has a documented LOSING REACH (Ozark Springs → Hwy 17, at the Narrows bend above Schlict Spring) where in low water flow drops from ~75 cfs above the Narrows to <30 cfs below as the river sinks underground — weed-choked, often walk-the-canoe. Water returns ~a mile downstream at Rockslide Bluff via Falling, Creasy, and Bartless Mill Springs. An upstream gauge reading can therefore badly OVERSTATE floatability through the losing reach; confirm the Hazelgreen gauge's position relative to the Narrows.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Spring-influenced but runoff-responsive; rises bring current, strainers and hazardous low-water bridges. NWS HZLM7 flood/action stages auto-fill at ingest.

localKnowledge: LOSING REACH (Al Agnew): Ozark Springs → Hwy 17, upper end of the Narrows bend above Schlict Spring — a large part of the river disappears underground in low water (~75 cfs above the Narrows, <30 cfs below). Put in at Schlict Spring in low water and it can be a barely-flowing, weed-choked trickle.

Rockslide Bluff: a 200 ft × 60 ft × 20 ft section of cliff collapsed into the river on 1971-11-03, still an interesting rapid; springs bubble up from the river bottom here and the flow (and floating) picks up markedly below it.
Longest river entirely within Missouri and the longest undammed river in the Ozarks; ~250 mi of floatable water. Floatable from ~Hwy E (≈11 mi below the highest put-in) down.

sections.name: Middle Gasconade (Jerome / Waynesville)
sections.description: The popular middle float reach around Jerome and Waynesville, represented by the Jerome gauge — the most-cited Gasconade float gauge.

representativeGauge.rationale: Jerome (06933500 / JRMM7) is the most-cited float gauge and the mid-river reference

thresholds.source: rivers.moherp.org Gasconade Jerome 06933500 page (owner-provided capture 2026-07-06): OBSERVED (community trip-calibrated) rating — Good begins at 400 cfs. The only observed anchor on this gauge; the site's ESTIMATED Good (1633) is model-derived and rejected per the calibration decision (a real trip floated Good at 475 cfs, ~4x below the model).
corroboratingSources: moherp trip reports rated Good at 475 cfs/1.36 ft (2012-08-18 Jerome->Whitehouse Ford, 3.0 mi), 1040 cfs/2.27 ft (2011-07-10 Little Piney Creek->Jerome, 1.2 mi), 1660 cfs/2.91 ft (2011-08-13 MO 28->Jerome, 10.8 mi)

publishedFloatTimes.source: rivers.moherp.org trip report (2011-08-13), Jerome gauge page
rivers.moherp.org trip report (2011-07-10), Jerome gauge page
rivers.moherp.org trip report (2012-08-18), Jerome gauge page

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN (numeric) — no gauge-keyed cfs floatability captured; generic guidance only. Capture a Jerome-keyed minimum.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Runoff-responsive; low-water bridges and strainers become hazards as water rises. NWS JRMM7 flood/action stages auto-fill at ingest. (Search-reported Jerome flood numbers were internally inconsistent — see openQuestions; do NOT use them.)

localKnowledge: High-quality smallmouth bass fishery; Jerome/Waynesville is the float hub (via search).

sections.name: Lower Gasconade (Rich Fountain toward the mouth)
sections.description: Lower reach represented by the Rich Fountain gauge, toward the Missouri River confluence. Larger, slower water.

representativeGauge.rationale: Rich Fountain (06934000 / RIFM7) is the lower-river reference gauge
representativeGauge.knownBias: larger, lower river — floatability semantics differ from the upper reaches

characteristics.lowWaterMeaning: UNKNOWN (numeric) — capture a Rich Fountain-keyed minimum; on the larger lower river low water mainly affects speed.
characteristics.risingWaterHazards: Rises arrive from the large upstream drainage. NWS RIFM7 flood/action stages auto-fill at ingest.

research.appRenderConfirmed: Eddy Gasconade page verified end-to-end 2026-07-06 (owner screenshot): aggregate streamflow 5,064 cfs (78% of normal), 3 gauge cards (max discharge Rich Fountain 2,450 cfs / 3.96 ft; highest-elevation Hazelgreen 845 ft), and daily trend chart all render on live USGS data.

flowStatisticsUsgs.note: VERIFIED USGS daily-mean statistics (July 6, seasonal), owner-provided 2026-07-06. DECISION 2026-07-06: percentile-derived floatability ladders are REJECTED as a threshold source — frequency statistics do not encode channel geometry (see black.json for the Bourbeuse falsification), and on this river the LOSING REACH makes gauge-vs-reality divergence even worse. Retain ONLY as context (% of normal, already rendered by the app) and sanity checks. Thresholds must come from the Agnew Gasconade article's per-reach 'Navigability' tables (the owner's paste was the intro narrative; the reach sections with Gradient/gauge/Normal flow/Navigability/Accesses follow further down the article) — that is the target source for all three reaches. Only the UPPER-reach (Hazelgreen) percentile set is on file; Jerome/Rich Fountain flows are 3–4× larger.

moherpRatings.note: rivers.moherp.org paddler gauge ratings (owner-provided 2026-07-06). OBSERVED = consolidated from community trip reports at known discharge (human/geometry-calibrated → encoded as thresholds at confidence medium). ESTIMATED = site model → reference only, per the calibration decision.

06933500_jerome.note: OBSERVED Good 400 encoded (optimal_min). Estimated Good 1633 is ~4x the observed and is rejected — a real trip floated Good at 475 cfs. Only observed anchor is Good; no observed Low/High yet.

06934000_richfountain.note: NO observed values on the moherp page → no thresholds encoded for the lower reach. Needs Agnew Navigability table or accumulated trip reports.

openQuestions: CALIBRATION CORE (UPPER + MID RESOLVED 2026-07-06): Hazelgreen ladder (Low 100 / Good 200 / High 1000 cfs) and Jerome optimal_min (Good 400 cfs) encoded from moherp OBSERVED community ratings, trip-corroborated, confidence medium. STILL MISSING: Rich Fountain (lower) ladder — its moherp page has NO observed values; needs the Agnew 'Navigability' table or accumulated trip reports. Jerome has only an observed Good (optimal_min); its Low/High anchors are still open. Agnew tables would also let Hazelgreen/Jerome confidences rise medium->high. Losing-reach datum: ~75 cfs above the Narrows / <30 cfs below in low water (hazard datum, not a float threshold).

ACCESS LANDMARKS from Agnew (no mileages/coords yet — [manual] capture): Hwy E (≈11 mi below the highest put-in), Hwy 17 at Waynesville, Hwy 28, Ozark Springs, Schlict Spring (in the losing reach), Jerome, Paydown (≈80 mi of lower river remain below Paydown). Build the full MCFA/MDC mileage table.
Reach division: 3 reaches drafted (Hazelgreen upper, Jerome mid, Rich Fountain lower); confirm boundaries and whether all three warrant separate calibration.
